content: Just want to point out that regarding Murphy, ||it's openly said that the previous carrier of the fetus (most likely Deborah's child) died and that Murphy was the next host after that. We're also directly told that Elton brought Murphy to the cult when Murphy was an infant, further proving no connection to Deborah's child. There shouldn't be any debate "is she or isn't she" there are 2 outright facts telling us that she isn't.||
||where does it say the previous carrier died?||
I don't remember specifically where, I just remember Murphy mentioning it (I think in one of the scenes before she encounters the keeper for the first time?)
in Murphy's flashback
legit baffled cuz this scene is literally throw at ur face

ok rereading, ||lily was meant to be the previous divine mother and the deborah thing definitely happened 5000 years ago. also murphy only gets picked because dissolution is damaging tulu's core. but she was born during the last astral alignment? so she can't be deborah's daughter? but then that means miryam never implanted tulu into deborah's daughter??? because she only had one chance. and murphy's actually from the temple light church??????????||
most logic conclusion is that debrorah daughter died bcuz she couldn't handle Tulu's bloodline
||hold on goliath straight up gives out murphy's backstory. how did i forget this.||
I mean she was also ripped out of her mother 3 months pre-mature. Realistically she wouldn't have survived infancy regardless
Miryam only interpet the revival ritual as "having a suitable host for the fetus" but the true nature is having Tulu's daughter host the fetus
iirc its also implied Miryam done this multiple times, meaning all of them before Murphy was a failure
but she says that the fetus can only survive one more attempt? cause it's so weak that if she implants it in the wrong person, it'll die
so either she implanted it in ||deborah's daughter, and she died yet the fetus lived somehow, or she never implanted it in deborah's daughter???||
She was most likely just wrong. She was wrong about a lot of things.
||what the fuckkkkk is the point of the ending of desecrate if deborah's daughter doesn't matter or mean anything||
or the fetus killed her daughter to prolong its lifespan
wait how is murphy tulu's daughter
like I said Miryam didn't understand the true nature of Tulu revival ritual either
she's Cthylla
oh, I thought u mean tlike
The story wasn't about how it ends, it was about showing Miryam's journey to become the person you encounter in the main story
canon/bloodline wise she was descended
literally the daughter Cthulhu born as his revive mechanic

right but then she would never have implanted the fetus in... or I guess what happened is she implanted it in deborah's daughter, it didn't work, but the fetus survived and miryam learns she can try and try again?
and they just don't bother to show us that i guess
I guesssssssss
but it's a lot of hullabaloo for deborah's daughter to not actually matter
cuz she already did this implanting multiple times
not before deborah's daughter no?
yea she's the first lmao
like she tries taking it into herself, it fails, and then she resolves that she only has one shot and the next host has to be perfect
i'm not against this version of events but I wish the writers had actually established it concretely
she theorized that the perfect host is children born when the stars are right
It demonstrated her insane fanatical dedication to the cause that she would kill her best friend for her child just to have an attempt at completing the ritual.
that's why she gave debrorah's daughter premature birth
she force the baby's birthdate to be the right time and then implanted fake Tulu into her
but the false god didn't summoned, meaning the ritual failed
there's a lot of ways you can demonstrate it/have her betray deborah without having to create a massive hanging plot thread that isn't resolved
but the fetus still exist all the way to AF315, meaning it indeed still survived
i mean hell they could have literally added one more scene where they implant it in deborah's fetus and it dies and miryam goes 'guess it's time to try again and again'
I wouldn't say it's unresolved in any way. The child was implanted with the seed, child died, Miryam continued her insane cause.
nah directly violate Debrorah is the best way to show how far gone Miryam is
right that's us guessing
and the plot already resolved, the ritual is a failure
in terms of what we actually know, anything could have happened to the fetus
they don't actually... show it
make much more sense than "murphy is debrorah daughter" nonsense
it's just a hugely important event done off screen that's not even mentioned

It's not really a guess, it's heavily implied and the only logical conclusion. Assuming that child even got to the point of having the seed implanted in it. It also could have died from the premature birth before ever implanting the seed.
it's as important as yet another failed Tulu reviving attempt
But the child themselves never meant anything in the story
you're saying CD is guessing but you give it too much thoughts
and completely missed the point
or what the story trying to imply
i'm not saying it doesn't
i'm saying from a storytelling perspective it's stupid to leave such a pivotal thing implied instead of explicitly presented
the fetus eating out the host so zero chance she would survive the ritual anw
when I say its a guess I just mean that it's not. on screen. directly shown
it's almost certainly what happened but they could technically write anything that happened there
because that's not the main focus of Miryam's dreamscape
In case you haven't noticed, a LOT of things aren't directly shown in this game. It's not meant to hold your hand through the story.
it's not about holding handddddd it's about the emotional payoff
arc 1 was written under the assumption that the players is in Lovecraft rabbit hole lol
it's the same reason gdoll's role in arc 1 sucks
we can easily infer what happened with her

but having it offscreen robs it of all the buildup/emotion

It was just random NPC that had a minor role in the story
you're trying to make a pebble a landscape
it's about the emotional payoff for miryam realizing that deborah's child didn't actually work and she killed deborah for nothing
then she try the next child so what?

she already believed that Tulu's prophercy is true

embeds.title: Samnite Wars
embeds.description:
The First, Second, and Third Samnite Wars (343–341 BC, 326–304 BC, and 298–290 BC) were fought between the Roman Republic and the Samnites, who lived on a stretch of the Apennine Mountains south of Rome and north of the Lucanian tribe.

The first of these wars was the result of Rome's intervention t
